description:
You are working as a release-manager who must perform a very quick DNS sanity-check before giving the green light for any deployment.

    Objective
    ----------
    1. Make sure a directory called “release” exists directly inside the user’s home directory:  
       /home/user/release
    2. Determine the IPv4 address that the local machine returns for the host-name “localhost”.
       • You may use any standard, non-interactive CLI tool that is normally available on a minimal Linux
         installation (e.g. “getent hosts localhost”, “host localhost”, or “dig +short localhost”).
       • You only need the first IPv4 address (do NOT record IPv6 results such as “::1”).
    3. Create the log file /home/user/release/dns_precheck.log containing **exactly** the two lines shown
       below, in the same order, with no leading/trailing blank spaces, and with a single UNIX newline
       character (“\n”) after the first line.  There must be **no newline** after the second line (i.e. the
       second line is the very last byte in the file).

       Line 1 →  localhost=127.0.0.1  
       Line 2 →  status=PASS

       Plain-text illustration of the required file content:

       localhost=127.0.0.1\n
       status=PASS

    4. Nothing else should be written to the file.  Do not add timestamps, headers, or extra blank lines.

    Acceptance Criteria (checked automatically)
    -------------------------------------------
    • /home/user/release/dns_precheck.log exists.  
    • The file contains exactly the two lines specified above, byte-for-byte, with the required newline placement.  
    • “localhost” resolves to “127.0.0.1” and you recorded that exact mapping in the log.  

    Hint:  
    A minimal solution usually involves just 2–3 shell commands:
      – one to create the directory,  
      – one to capture the IPv4 address of “localhost”, and  
      – one to write/overwrite the log file with the two required lines.
truth:
Expected final state for verification:

    1. Directory: /home/user/release
       • Must exist and be readable by the regular user.

    2. File: /home/user/release/dns_precheck.log
       • Must exist.
       • File permissions: 0644 or more permissive (user-writable, world-readable is fine).
       • Exact byte sequence (shown with \n to denote newline):
         “localhost=127.0.0.1\nstatus=PASS”

         ┌── hexdump ‑C representation ──┐
         | 00000000  6c 6f 63 61 6c 68 6f 73  74 3d 31 32 37 2e 30 2e  |localhost=127.0.|
         | 00000010  30 2e 31 0a 73 74 61 74  75 73 3d 50 41 53 53       |0.1.status=PASS|
         | 0000001f                                                    |
         └──────────────────────────────┘

    3. No other new files should be present inside /home/user/release.

    The automated test will:
       • stat /home/user/release/dns_precheck.log
       • read-file and compare to the exact expected two-line content
       • fail if extra lines, different casing, extra whitespace, or missing newline after the first line are observed.
